title: Fields of the World — Global
description:
Global agricultural field boundaries and prediction-quality layers at 10 m resolution — ~1.6 billion field polygons per year (2024 & 2025) across 195 countries — produced by applying the [PRUE field-boundary segmentation model](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.27101) to global Sentinel-2 mosaics ([Robinson et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.11055)). Part of [Fields of the World](https://fieldsofthe.world); published openly under CC-BY-4.0 by the [Taylor Geospatial Institute](https://taylorgeospatial.org/) and collaborators. Dataset home page: [source.coop/ftw/global-data](https://source.coop/ftw/global-data). Background reading: the [global field-boundary map](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.11055), the [Fields of the World benchmark](https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.16252), the [PRUE recipe](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.27101), and the [FTW field guide](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08131).

The data itself (billions of polygons, COGs, and Zarr — hundreds of GB) lives on [Source Cooperative](https://source.coop/ftw/global-data); the STAC / [Portolan](https://portolan-sdi.org) **catalog metadata** is managed in the [ftw-data-catalog GitHub repository](https://github.com/fieldsoftheworld/ftw-data-catalog). Pull requests to improve the catalog are welcome there.
